channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: KEHPHPU raises alarm over unqualified staff managing Nairobi Food Certification Unit
item.description: The Kenya Environmental Health and Public Health Practitioners’ Union (KEHPHPU) has raised serious concerns over the management of the Food Hygiene and Medical Certification Unit at Nairobi City Hall....

item.title: Over 3,000 Runners Take Part in Absa Dar City Marathon Supporting Healthcare
item.description: Dar es Salaam hosted the 2026 Absa Dar City Marathon yesterday, drawing more than 3,000 runners from across Tanzania. Sponsored by Absa Bank Tanzania, the event featured 5km, 10km, and 21km races, att...

item.title: Peel police rescue 57 kittens and 8 cats, arrest man on multiple animal cruelty charges
item.description: Peel Regional Police, in cooperation with Animal Welfare Services, rescued 57 kittens and eight cats following an investigation into alleged animal cruelty. The investigation began after Animal Welfar...

item.title: IFP Expels Sipho 'Es' Mbatha Over Alleged Extortion
item.description: The Inkatha Freedom Party (IFP) has officially expelled Mr Sipho 'Es' Mbatha, formerly a councillor in the King Cetshwayo District Municipality, following allegations of misconduct. According to the I...

item.title: A Retrospective of the UK’s Most Unsuccessful Eurovision Entries
item.description: The United Kingdom has endured a challenging history in the Eurovision Song Contest, with numerous entries failing to capture the audience's favour. The most recent humiliation occurred in 2026 when L...

item.title: Federal High Court Grants Bail to Former Kaduna Governor El-Rufai with Strict Conditions
item.description: Federal High Court for Abuja don grant bail to Malam Nasir El-Rufai, wey be former Kaduna State governor. The court fix the bail at N100 million plus one surety wey gots to be equal in value. Justice ...

item.title: Hai District gets new tower to improve mobile network coverage
item.description: Hai District in Tanzania has finally overcome its long-standing communication difficulties thanks to the construction of a new telecommunication tower. Residents of the district had been facing challe...

item.title: Oyo Teacher Killed During Kidnapping Incident, Others Still Missing
item.description: People for Oriire Local Government Area, Oyo State, don dey mourn as one of their own, Michael Oyedokun, teacher for Community High School, Ahoro-Esinele, don lose him life after he was kidnapped. Acc...

item.title: Candace Montgomery’s life after her 1980 acquittal and career in therapy
item.description: Candace Montgomery, now living under her maiden name Candace Wheeler in Georgia, has led a largely private life since her 1980 acquittal for the murder of Betty Gore. At the time, Montgomery, a housew...

item.title: Malaysia’s Prime Minister Consults King Over Potential Early Polls
item.description: Malaysia’s Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim met with King Sultan Ibrahim at the national palace on Monday, amid speculation of an early general election. While the next general election is not due until e...

item.title: Court Orders Government to Disclose SGR Contracts and Related Documents
item.description: The Court of Appeal in Kenya has upheld a High Court ruling requiring the government to release contracts and associated documents for the Standard Gauge Railway (SGR) project. This decision comes aft...

item.title: Thousands attend Trump-backed Christian prayer rally on National Mall to rededicate US to God
item.description: Thousands of people gathered on the National Mall in Washington on Sunday for a daylong prayer rally aimed at rededicating the United States as 'One Nation under God'. The event, backed by President D...

item.title: Stalin Criticises Vijay Over Governance and Law-and-Order Concerns in Tamil Nadu
item.description: Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam (DMK) president MK Stalin on Monday criticised Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK) leader Joseph Vijay, claiming he became Tamil Nadu Chief Minister by influencing children on Ins...

item.title: Navy Seizes P526.2 Million of Smuggled Cigarettes in Mindanao
item.description: In a series of coordinated naval operations in the territorial waters of the Bangsamoro region, the Philippine Navy successfully seized imported cigarettes valued at a total of P526.2 million. Two sep...

item.title: Gauteng sees over 69,000 new hypertension cases, many under 45
item.description: Gauteng Health has raised concern over a notable increase in hypertension cases, with more than 69,000 new diagnoses recorded in the 2025/26 financial year. Alarmingly, over 26,000 adults under the ag...

item.title: Katharine Hepburn’s 1937 Photo by Howard Hughes and Personal Letters Go on Sale
item.description: A rare photograph of Katharine Hepburn taken by famed aviator and filmmaker Howard Hughes around 1937 has recently resurfaced along with some of her personal love letters. These items, originating fro...

item.title: Uganda passes law to monitor foreign funding and regulate political groups
item.description: The Ugandan President, Yoweri Museveni, don sign one law wey go monitor foreign aid to opposition groups for di country. Government talk say di law go help dem protect Uganda from foreign interference...

item.title: Absa Marathon promotes fitness while supporting community health
item.description: The Absa Marathon in Tanzania is more than just a race; it’s a community-focused event that combines athletics with healthcare awareness and support. Participants not only engage in running competitio...

item.title: Teen TikTok Star Sana Yousaf Murder Case: Accused Denies All Charges, Alleges Police Misconduct
item.description: In Islamabad, the main accused in the murder of 17-year-old TikTok influencer Sana Yousaf, Umar Hayat, has withdrawn his earlier confession before the trial court, claiming that police falsely implica...

item.title: Indian PM Modi Encourages Citizens to Conserve Fuel and Adopt Remote Work Amid Energy Challenges
item.description: Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi has called on citizens to adopt energy-saving measures, urging them to work from home, reduce travel, and minimize fuel consumption amid surging energy prices cause...
